title: Cannot Create New Container With -cpu-shares= Using cgroups v1 and cgroups v2 on Raspbian Lite
body:
/kind bug



<!--
Creating new podman containers fails if --cpu-shares is passed as a creation argument. This error occurs in both cgroups v1 and cgroups v2
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a new container with run or create and pass --cpu-shares argument with cgroups v1. The following error will be obtained: 
`Error: container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:424: container init caused \"process_linux.go:390: setting cgroup config for procHooks process caused \\\"cannot set cpu limit: container could not join or create cgroup\\\"\"": OCI runtime error`

2. Load cgroups v2 and retry the run command and the following error will be obtained: 
"Error: error creating systemd unit `libpod-6b1b19071aa0f8c1592fc8a6f77e3d429f46a91fc97bc6bc086b7aeb6919bba3.scope`: got `failed`: OCI runtime error" 

3. Create the container without --cpu-shares and the container will be successfully created. 


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Running Raspbian Lite on RPi3B+

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 02:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Yes it works swimmingly well with sudo. Now how do I make it work with rootless? I've already added 
```
[Service]
Delegate=memory pids cpu io
```
to `/etc/systemd/system/user@1000.service.d/delegate.conf`
and 
```
[Service]
Delegate=yes
```
with `systemctl edit user@1000.service`
Why isn't that enough?

what is the error you get on cgroup v2?

On cgroup v1 it won't work.  Even if the cgroup is chowned, we don't support it in Podman, delegation to unprivileged users is not safe

Hello. Sorry for the delay, I ran into a few snafus that I had to address before being able to interact with podman again. In any case, the error yielded would be:
`Error: unable to start container "370879258ccd4e823ccec806fc3257c9339882fc24dbac99fdd047c3824b080e": sd-bus call: Permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error`

Not only that but when mounting cgroups v2 (whether on boot or afterwards), running any container would yield an error such as running an already established container in this example:
`Error: unable to start container "a437f0c790a981af5524921297dcb95df08fe30931fb5a05df166eef3c65fb5b": systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available: OCI runtime error
`
This and other issues have gotten me thinking that Debian (Or Raspbian) are not ideal for working with podman. I'm preparing Fedora IoT for installation instead. Hopefully this will fix various issues and not just this one.

it looks like systemd is not available or it is too old.

Can you run something like `unshare -r systemd-run --scope --user echo hi`?

Output:

`Failed to create bus connection: No such file or directory`

systemd version:
```
apt list systemd
Listing... Done
systemd/buster-backports,now 245.6-1~bpo10+1 armhf [installed]
```
I'm closing this issue as I've switched to Fedora IoT and everything works fine on it. It is probably an issue with how debian handles cgroups v2.
